title: The White Moon Wasteth.
content:
The white moon wasteth,
    And cold morn hasteth
        Athwart the snow,
    The red east burneth
    And the tide turneth,
        And thou must go.

    Think not, sad rover,
    Their story all over
        Who come from far - 
    Once, in the ages
    Won goodly wages
        Led by a star.

    Once, for all duly
    Guidance doth truly
        Shine as of old,
    Opens for me and thee
    Once, opportunity
        Her gates of gold.

    Enter, thy star is out,
    Traverse nor faint nor doubt
        Earth's antres wild,
    Thou shalt find good and rest
    As found the Magi blest
        That divine Child.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Jean Ingelow delivers a powerful performance in "The White Moon Wasteth."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
